title: Exploring Dragon Poems and Poetic Forms

questions.question: What does "lurking" mean?
options: English that follows all the usual grammar rules and forms complete sentences.
hiding and waiting for someone or something, often in a secret or sneaky way.
questions.correct_answer: hiding and waiting for someone or something, often in a secret or sneaky way.
questions.explanation: "lurking" means: hiding and waiting for someone or something, often in a secret or sneaky way.

questions.question: What does "Standard English" mean?
options: hiding and waiting for someone or something, often in a secret or sneaky way.
English that follows all the usual grammar rules and forms complete sentences.
questions.correct_answer: English that follows all the usual grammar rules and forms complete sentences.
questions.explanation: "Standard English" means: English that follows all the usual grammar rules and forms complete sentences.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Poems can create vivid impressions of ______ (like dragons) using just a few words.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Poems can create vivid impressions of characters (like dragons) using just a few words."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Haikus are very short poems, often focusing on a single image.
Tankas are a bit longer than haikus and can tell more of a story.
Poems can create vivid impressions of characters (like dragons) using just a few words.
Standard English means using proper grammar and complete sentences, even when expanding poetry.
questions.correct_answer: Haikus are very short poems, often focusing on a single image.
questions.explanation: Correct! "Haikus are very short poems, often focusing on a single image." is one of the key things to remember.
